briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for May 25, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Crisis Warnings and the Urgency of Adaptation
sections.content: Recent reports emphasize the urgency of climate adaptation for the upcoming summer [4, 10], urging proactive responses to the issue of sea-level rise due to thermal expansion of seawater [9] and changing patterns of pest and disease outbreaks due to climate change [3, 5]. In response, Incheon City is operating customized defense systems to prevent agricultural, livestock, and fishery disasters during the summer [10, 11]. Internationally, parts of the UK have reached a heatwave threshold, recording their highest May temperatures in 80 years [62], and a climate change risk report has exposed significant public health policy gaps [55]. Southeast Asia's leading economy is pursuing a 600km dike project to combat climate change, and it is emphasized that China and ASEAN should prioritize cooperation to address extreme climate events [49, 61].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Practices and Policy Challenges
sections.content: Efforts to achieve carbon neutrality goals are continuing across various local governments in Korea. Gwangju City exceeded its greenhouse gas reduction target last year, achieving a 94% carbon neutrality goal attainment rate [19, 22, 24], and Seongdong-gu achieved 93.2% of its greenhouse gas reduction target, receiving an 'Excellent' rating in the 2025 carbon neutrality and climate crisis adaptation measures implementation review [15, 16, 17, 32, 37]. Jeongeup City is promoting changes across roads, industries, and administration to transition into a carbon-neutral city [29], and Gurye County held a basic plan briefing for the 2026 Carbon-Neutral Soil Revitalization Expo [25]. Despite these positive movements, the amendment to the Carbon Neutrality Act, which was promised for May processing in the National Assembly, has been postponed once again, leaving a sense of regret [12, 27].

sections.title: 🌱 Innovative Technologies and Accelerated Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: The development of innovative technologies and the transition to renewable energy are accelerating in response to climate change. Specific reduction efforts are underway, such as technology for rapidly detecting methane, a major contributor to climate change, using AI [1], and the development of methane-reducing feed for the Hanwoo cattle industry [21]. Gyeongsangbuk-do has also commenced the development of underground smart farms using new materials [2]. Competition for 'pyrolysis oil' technology development is fierce in the era of high oil prices and to achieve carbon neutrality goals [14], and financial sector capital flows are also shifting towards artificial intelligence and renewable energy sectors [40]. Korea's largest-scale Gimcheon green hydrogen production is commencing with renewable energy [41], and efforts to expand the renewable energy base are active, with Hanwha Q CELLS supplying domestic cells and modules for large-scale solar power projects [47]. Power generation companies, ahead of mergers and acquisitions, are also staking their survival on securing renewable energy as a 'green asset' [45].

sections.title: 🌍 International Trends: Spectrum of Climate Change Awareness and Response
sections.content: The international community is showing diverse movements in its awareness and response to climate change. While news has emerged that the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) has discarded the worst-case climate change scenarios [53], fundamental questions are also being raised, such as 'Which is worse for climate change: landfill methane or automotive carbon dioxide?' [51]. Restoration efforts are also underway, including discussions on the potential use of concrete formwork to revive coral reefs destroyed by bombs and climate change [50]. The New Zealand government has provided certainty to its climate change tort law [57], and in the UK, applications for the 'Local Climate Change and Nature Fund' have opened, encouraging local-level responses [56].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the severity of the climate crisis along with various domestic and international response efforts. This is a time that requires continuous attention and effort. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: Highest temperatures of 2026 in England, Wales and Northern Ireland as Kew Gardens in London reaches 32.3C England, Wales and Northern Ireland recorded their highest temperatures of 2026 on Sunday, which was also the UK’s hottest May day for at least 79 years.Kew Gardens in west London recorded 32.3C (90.1F), Cardiff 27.4C and Armagh 23.4C. Continue reading...

summary.phenomenon: On Sunday, 2026, England, Wales, and Northern Ireland recorded their highest temperatures of the year. This also marked the UK's hottest May in at least 79 years. London's Kew Gardens reached 32.3C, Cardiff 27.4C, and Armagh 23.4C.
